channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Karnataka to link excise duty to alcohol content: Will liquor get cheaper?
One week after the Iran war, who is winning?
Who is Balen Shah, 35-year-old former rapper likely to be next Nepal PM?
Iran war: Could Dubai run out of fresh food in 10 days?
‘F**k India, China’ and more: The many controversies of Balen Shah, Nepal’s likely PM
Iran war: Why are wealthy Indians moving their money to Singapore?
How Trump’s contradictions on Iran may open a Pandora’s box in West Asia
item.description: The prospect of strife in Iran in which groups are pitted against one another and forces emerge which weaken central authority, will have grave consequences for the region. This may not immediately trouble either the US or Israel but can disrupt the flow of events in the Arabian Peninsula

item.title: Nepal polls: Gen Z wave sweeps old guard as Balen Shah heads for historic mandate
item.description: Balen Shah appears to be achieving what no Nepali leader has in three decades—securing a two-thirds majority. The real challenge now is delivering on promises and shedding Nepal’s 'basket case' image in South Asia

item.title: India’s tightrope walk: Preserving strategic autonomy amid the Iran war
item.description: India must perform a delicate balancing act to maintain its critical defence and technology ties with Israel while simultaneously protecting its long-term energy and trade interests with Iran

item.title: How Israel-Iran rivalry is turning from strategic to civilisational
item.description: The Israel-Iran conflict is not merely a tactical contest between two states pursuing strategic interests. It is a conflict embedded in theology, identity, and historical memory

item.title: How Pakistan’s West Asia ‘balancing act’ turned into diplomatic clownery
item.description: Pakistan’s statements during the ongoing crisis in West Asia give the impression of a government that was somersaulting in its response to every new development, yet running like a headless chicken, completely directionless in its diplomatic consistency

item.title: Nepal’s ballot revolt: Voters seek change after years of political musical chairs
item.description: The mood across the country is to reject traditional parties such as the Nepali Congress, CPN UML and CPN Maoist for failing to curb corruption and deliver jobs and good governance — giving the 'Gen Z'-backed Rashtriya Swatantra Party a fair chance
